title: bindings: manifest should follow `es_model` naming convention while marshalling `OSVersion` and `OSFeatures`
body:
It seems API needs json names for `OSVersion` and `OSFeatures` in `es_model`
ref: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/domain/entities/manifest.go#L42

So at bindings end ensure that we honor `es_model` naming convention when
we perform marshaling otherwise API will ignore these fields

Closes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12774

comments.body:

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM
This is a breaking API change. I want to research why renaming the keys corrects the json decoding...
@jwhonce I think `os_version` and `os_features` in annotation is just being ignored by go deserializer, I'm not sure if these fields ever worked so it might not break users.
If @jwhonce is happy with this change, then LGTM.  I'm just concerned about the breakage issue he raised.
@flouthoc @jwhonce What is going on with this one?
@rhatdan I'm waiting for @jwhonce 's confirmation on this one.
@jwhonce Just a friendly ping.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@jwhonce If something is changed in upstream then it makes sense to me. But I can just drop the field changes test should still pass right.
I'm rebasing and just adding test instead of field change if test passes it closes the issue on upstream I guess

Pending test results

/hold
/lgtm

> @flouthoc Below is a script showing the API working as is against main using existing field names. Do you agree we can close this and **not** change the field names?
> 
> ```
> #!/bin/bash -xe
> 
> SOCK="--unix-socket /run/user/$UID/podman/podman.sock"
> 
> curl -XDELETE $SOCK http://d/v4.0.1/libpod/manifests/foo
> curl -XPOST $SOCK http://d/v4.0.1/libpod/manifests/create/foo
> 
> curl -XPUT $SOCK http://d/v4.0.1/libpod/manifests/foo \
>   -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
>   -d '{"Operation":"update", "os_version":"7.7.7", "Images": [ "docker://k8s.gcr.io/pause@sha256:f365626a556e58189fc21d099fc64603db0f440bff07f77c740989515c544a39" ]}'
> 
> curl -XGET $SOCK http://d/v4.0.1/libpod/manifests/foo/json |jq
> ```
> 
> Output:
> 
> ```
> {
>   "schemaVersion": 2,
>   "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json",
>   "manifests": [
>     {
>       "mediaType": "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json",
>       "size": 527,
>       "digest": "sha256:f365626a556e58189fc21d099fc64603db0f440bff07f77c740989515c544a39",
>       "platform": {
>         "architecture": "amd64",
>         "os": "linux",
>         "os.version": "7.7.7"
>       }
>     }
>   ]
> }
> ```

@jwhonce Found the issue it seems becuase of https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/domain/entities/manifest.go#L42 API wants paramters in es_model like `os_version` and `os_features` as you have tried in example but bindings does not follow this convention and instead marshals as `OSVersion` and `OSVersion` which is ignored by API. 
Adding similar name scheme at bindings end should fix this. I have amended the PR PTAL
@jwhonce PTAL test is green now and fix is in bindings and not in API so should not be a breaking change anymore.

LGTM
but I'd like @jwhonce to take one more spin thru.  Happy green test buttons.
/lgtm
@jwhonce friendly ping :)

/hold cancel
Statute of limitations has passed on this one.
